graph & maps of content

The graph.

Every note, wired by the links it already has — parents edges and same-directory references. Bigger circles are the hubs: the notes most of the Atlas points at. Hover to trace a note's neighborhood; click to open it. The 46 notes, 47 links.

kind reference feature analysis bug
maturity seedling budding evergreen
edge parent reference

Maps of Content

The hubs, ranked by how many notes point at them. Each lists its cluster — the notes that reference it. No hand-curated index: these fall straight out of the links the Atlas already has.

Electricity — identified, and their progress 10 in · 10 links

A tracker for kolu's electricities — infrastructure pulled out from beneath the app into its own thing — and where each stands.

  • @kolu/surface-daemon — one spine for kaval and odu serve feature
  • @kolu/surface-mcp — every surface an MCP server, honestly scoped feature
  • A CI runner you attach to — graduating mini-ci beyond justci feature
  • Code browser preview → @kolu/solid-fileview feature
  • Is Mobile Electricity? analysis
  • odu-runner — a runner you can reach with nothing running feature
  • surface-app — the app shell for surface apps reference
  • surface-connection — the WebSocket connection, owned upstream feature
  • The Code tab is a browser → @kolu/solid-browser feature
  • The kolu Atlas reference
R-4 — kaval, the standalone PTY daemon 7 in · 7 links

The redo plan for R-4 — local PTY daemonization, reframed: the daemon is kaval, a standalone program in the drishti/odu tradition, and kolu is its first client. One rule (package boundary = process boundary = staleKey hash), a dumb fully-specified wire, and four PRs — the spawn-policy inversion, the kaval binary + client, the door with survival off, then survival — each complete w.r.t. the hazards it opens. Carries the #1034 postmortem and its hard constraints.

  • @kolu/surface-daemon — one spine for kaval and odu serve feature
  • ChromeBar identity rail (srv · client · kaval) feature
  • Ghostex vs. kolu remote-terminals analysis
  • Kaval PTY-host heap OOM analysis
  • kolu-tui — a CLI terminal client for kolu-server feature
  • odu-runner — a runner you can reach with nothing running feature
  • Remote terminals over SSH feature
Code browser preview → @kolu/solid-fileview 5 in · 6 links

Invent the grid, slim the house — a Source ⇄ Rendered file-view toggle built on reusable leaf packages. Markdown is the first lit toggle; HTML/SVG next.

  • Cite → open in the Code browser feature
  • Code-tab filter strands empty directories bug
  • Git-status indicators in the Code-tab "All files" view feature
  • Markdown preview: Obsidian-style [[wikilinks]] feature
  • Markdown preview: repo-relative links open the target file bug
Kaval sessions — dial daemons, multiplex the canvas 4 in · 4 links

A direction reframe for R-2 now kaval ships. Dial kavals as first-class endpoints — local over a unix socket, remote over ssh (provisioned via surface-nix-host) — with hosts auto-detected from ssh config. The same substrate supports either switching one kaval at a time (tmux-style) or multiplexing several on one canvas (per-tile piggybacks on its kaval); the canvas metaphor leans multiplex. First step — kaval-tui create, then --host (reach + provision in one PR).

  • arivu — terminal awareness as an ephemeral surface on kaval feature
  • kaval-tui that roams — remote attach that survives the network analysis
  • kolu-tui — a CLI terminal client for kolu-server feature
  • Remote terminals over SSH feature
odu-runner — a runner you can reach with nothing running 4 in · 5 links

Phase 0 of odu-web, owned by the runner: split serve from run so the socket outlives any single pipeline, give runs an identity a ledger can hold (repo × sha × seq × node), and decide who keeps the long-lived coordinator alive — priced against the warm pu-box pool. Everything odu-web consumes; nothing odu-web should have to build.

  • @kolu/surface-daemon — one spine for kaval and odu serve feature
  • A CI runner you attach to — graduating mini-ci beyond justci feature
  • odu-web — the service face of odu feature
  • R-4 — kaval, the standalone PTY daemon feature
surface-app — the app shell for surface apps 4 in · 4 links

A library for the class of @kolu/surface app that is really a desktop application you run against your own server — fresh delivery, server/build identity, the connection/update lifecycle, and desktop install, all owned by one shell.

  • A CI runner you attach to — graduating mini-ci beyond justci feature
  • Electricity — identified, and their progress analysis
  • odu-web — the service face of odu feature
  • surface-connection — the WebSocket connection, owned upstream feature
